sections.paragraph:
prop. part. Hiph. (from the root שָׁחַת) destroying, that which destroys, hence-

(1) destruction; Exo 12:13 Eze 5:16 21:36 , חָרָשֵׁי מַשְׁחִית “artizans of destruction;” Eze 25:15.

(2) a snare, a noose, Jer 5:26, compare מֹוקֵשׁ; hence soldiers lying in wait, 1Sa 14:15. הַר הַמַּשְׁחִית (the mount of destruction, or of the snare)-(a) Mount Olivet, on account of the idols there worshipped which would be destruction to the people, and-(b) for the same reason the name was given to Babylon, Jer 51:25.
